compiled_state.title: As a validation process, I can score design fidelity and gate story closure
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: pixelmatch compares Figma PNG exports against Playwright screenshots, produces a Design Fidelity Score (0-100), applies threshold-based tagging, and degrades gracefully when tools are unavailable.
**Why**: Provides an automated quality gate that catches visual regressions before stories are marked complete, while never blocking closure when infrastructure is unavailable.
**Scope**:
- IN: pixelmatch comparison (threshold 0.1), fidelity score computation (matching/total pixels x 100), highlighted diff image generation, threshold gating (design.figma_fidelity_threshold, default 85), design:fidelity_verified and design:fidelity_gap tagging, graceful degradation (Playwright absent or 429 → warning + advisory ticket + skip), frame-to-screenshot pairing via shared identifier from merged manifest
- OUT: Sprint report integration for fidelity tags (deferred — tracked as follow-up; tag exists but sprint skill consumer update is out of scope for this epic)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, pixelmatch compares paired Figma PNGs and Playwright screenshots (matched via shared identifier from the merged manifest) and produces a Design Fidelity Score (matching pixels / total pixels x 100) with a highlighted diff image written to designs/{uuid}/validation/
  ← Satisfies: "produces a Design Fidelity Score (0-100) using perceptual diff"
- When this story is complete, score >= threshold (design.figma_fidelity_threshold, default 85) tags the story design:fidelity_verified; score < threshold tags design:fidelity_gap
  ← Satisfies: "below threshold → design:fidelity_gap"
- When this story is complete, if Playwright is not installed or REST API returns 429, a warning is logged with specific remediation guidance, an advisory ticket is created to track the skipped validation, and story closure is not blocked
  ← Satisfies: "graceful degradation — never blocks story closure"
- When this story is complete, frames with no corresponding screenshot (partial coverage) are logged as warnings, not errors — fidelity score is computed only for matched pairs
  ← Satisfies: adversarial review finding on partial coverage handling
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] Threshold calibration: default 85 accounts for sub-pixel rendering differences; document expected variance range and provide calibration guidance
- [Architecture] Frame-to-screenshot pairing needs a shared identifier scheme (e.g., Figma node ID mapped to route/component via merged manifest). Specify which manifest field is used.
- [Consumer Impact] Sprint report surfacing of design:fidelity_gap is deferred — the tag exists on the ticket but sprint skill has no consumer code for it yet. Track as follow-up.
- [Reliability] Advisory ticket created on graceful degradation ensures skipped validations don't go unnoticed

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
